Why_It_Belongs_in_Survey: The paper places an LLM planner inside an RL training or execution loop. This directly targets exploration efficiency, tool-interactive decision quality, which fits an LLM-in-the-RL-loop survey scope.

Title: Keep CALM and Explore: Language Models for Action Generation in Text-based Games
Authors: Shunyu Yao, Rohan Rao, Matthew Hausknecht, Karthik Narasimhan
Contribution_Summary: Text-based games present a unique challenge for autonomous agents to operate in natural language and handle enormous action spaces. In this paper, we propose the Contextual Action Language Model (CALM) to generate a compact set of action candidates at each game state. We evaluate our approach using the Jericho benchmark, on games unseen by CALM during training.
Why_It_Belongs_in_Survey: The paper places an LLM/VLM reward and feedback module inside an RL training or execution loop. This directly targets exploration efficiency, reward quality for policy optimization, which fits an LLM-in-the-RL-loop survey scope.
